our_open_deliverables.text: Send the final executable contractual package (MSA, OF1, OF2) for Publicis' final review
Complete and submit the Publicis OneTrust vendor security declaration

buyer_dependent_items.text: Attend the 2026-07-06 review call and validate functional scope to finalise OF2/SoW
Provide Zycus legal OneTrust access to complete the vendor declaration
Return final legal comments to reach the executable MSA version

competitive_position.summary: Sole-source: Publicis selected Zycus without an RFP, on a partner introduction and Florence's prior PwC deployment, so there is no external competitor to displace. The current threat is internal, not a rival: (1) de-scoping Merlin for iContract from core to an option, a roughly $50K TCV erosion under active negotiation (highest current value threat, 2026-06-29); and (2) status-quo / manual-process inertia, which is low given the confirmed pain and Florence's year-end quick-win mandate. No new external competitor surfaced this sweep. We protect value by keeping Merlin core, anchored to the contract-visibility pain and the FY26 go-live.

competitors.name: Internal de-scoping: Merlin core to option (FY26 budget pressure)
competitors.quote: Emeline she did not came back on Merlin for I Contract to be put as option instead of core module which would have a significant impact (50K if we consider discount) on TCV (let's see if Florence will insist or not)
competitors.how_we_win: Keep Merlin as core by tying its contract-intelligence and agentic-intake value to the stated pain (visibility, process mess) and the quick-win go-live; secure Florence's explicit sign-off before OF1 is frozen.

competitors.name: Status quo / manual contract process
competitors.quote: Actual contractual process is a mess / Email validation / No dashboard / No possibility to know easily what are the next steps.
competitors.how_we_win: Centralise CLM (iContract), automate request/approval (iRequest), and deliver contract intelligence via Merlin; Florence's FY26 quick-win urgency keeps do-nothing risk low.

champion_strength.source: OpportunityContactRole (Florence, Decision Maker); SF activity contact (Emeline Le Foyer); Next Step 2026-06-29; Email 2026-07-01
champion_strength.summary: Two-tier champion structure, strengthening this sweep. Florence Tinsley-Roy (CPO, Decision Maker) is the strategic champion who selected Zycus sole-source on her PwC track record and set the FY26 quick-win mandate; Emeline Le Foyer (Global IT Procurement Director) is the operational champion driving daily execution, negotiation, and OF1 iterations, and pulls Zycus for calls. Since last sweep the internal blockers that flagged champion risk cleared: legal resolved, functional doc delivered, and Stephen Cook and Paras re-engaged for the 2026-07-06 workshop. What remains is Florence's explicit sign-off on the final commercial package and on keeping Merlin core.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Florence Tinsley-Roy, Chief Procurement Officer, Decision Maker, PRIMARY
Email 2026-06-24 Updated Commercial Proposal to Florence and Emeline
Email 2026-06-24 John Woodcock (VP EMEA) catch-up with Florence
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: Florence Tinsley-Roy, Chief Procurement Officer, is the Economic Buyer and Decision Maker (OpportunityContactRole, PRIMARY, Role=Decision Maker) and the named budget owner. She made the sole-source vendor selection and carries a year-end mandate to show procurement impact. Her authority showed when the Updated Commercial Proposal was addressed directly to her (2026-06-24) and Zycus VP EMEA John Woodcock arranged to catch up with her the next day ('Looking forward to catching up tomorrow', 2026-06-24). Day-to-day commercial and legal negotiation is run by Emeline Le Foyer (procurement), but final sign-off authority sits with the relevant stakeholder direct footprint on execution detail is light, so confirming her explicit sign-off on the final package and on Merlin scope is the priority.

recommended_moves: Have John Woodcock confirm with Florence that Merlin for iContract stays core.
Finalise OF2/SoW scope with Stephen Cook and Paras on the 6 July review call.
Complete and submit the Publicis OneTrust vendor security declaration.
